REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. THE OPERATIVE CONTRACT PROVIDES FOR "REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS." OPPOSING PARTY CITES NO AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT THE LENDER MAY MAKE A UNILATERAL DECISION OF THE REASONABLENESS OF THE FEES AND COSTS AND DEMAND THEIR PAYMENT IN ORDER TO REINSTATE THE LOAN. Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jan-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jason Estavillo, Esq. (510-982-3001) for Plaintiff Ondyn Herschelle.
Marianne Disckson, Esq. (415-397-2823) for Defendant Pilgrim REO & Capital Crossing Serving Company.

The Court announces the issue of the case re: argument on attorney's fees. Process is not Correct. Defendant ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction is GRANTED. The operative contract provides for "reasonable attorney's fees and costs." Opposing party cites no authority for the proposition that the lender may make a unilateral decision of the reasonableness of the fees and costs and demand their payment in order to reinstate the loan. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported (D501)
